session.user:
# /erk:plan-implement
Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan) 2. Executing the implementation 3. Running CI and submitting the PR
## Prerequisites
- Must be in a git repository managed by erk - GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated - One of: - A plan number, URL, or file path argument - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`) - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-implement # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan /erk:plan-implement 2521 # Fetch and implement plan #2521 /erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 # URL form /erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md # Implement from local markdown file ```
---
## CRITICAL: PR Operations
**NEVER** run direct GitHub CLI commands for PR operations during implementation:
- `gh pr edit` — destroys plan-header metadata block - `gh pr ready` — bypasses lifecycle tracking - `gh pr close` / `gh pr merge`
All PR operations are handled by `erk pr submit` in Step 12. It automatically:
- Generates the PR title and body - Preserves the plan-header metadata block - Marks draft PRs as ready for review - Updates lifecycle stage
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
Parse `8586` and run the consolidated setup command:
- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>` - **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
```bash erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>] ```
This single command handles:
- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR) - Setting up from a local markdown file - Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup) - Creating/checking out the feature branch - Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content - Running impl-init validation - Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)
Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:
1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title` 2. Generate a branch slug from the title: - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc" 3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:
Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`
Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
Parse the JSON output to get `plan_number`, then:
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <plan_number> ```
The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).
If this fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:
- Overall goal and context - Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls) - Implementation phases and dependencies - Success criteria
**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.
### Step 3: Load Related Documentation
If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.
### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries
Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.
### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started
```bash erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:
- The content has been saved to GitHub - The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/` - Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save
### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially
For each phase:
1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite) 2. **Read task requirements** carefully 3. **Implement code AND tests together**: - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns - Follow project AGENTS.md standards 4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite) 5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next
**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation
### Step 7: Report Progress
After each phase: report changes made and what's next.
### Step 8: Final Verification
Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.
### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended
```bash erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true ```
### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
### Step 11: Run CI Iteratively
1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions 2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
**CRITICAL**: Never delete the impl directory - leave for user review (no auto-commit).
### Step 12: Submit PR
Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
```bash erk pr submit ```
This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:
```bash erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true ```
Then validate PR completion invariants:
```bash erk pr check --stage=impl ```
This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks (e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
### Step 13: Output Format
- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..." - **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..." - **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes - **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"
---
## Related Commands
- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow) - `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state
session.assistant: I'll start by setting up the implementation for plan #8586.
Implementation environment set up. Let me read the plan.
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Always Suggest Save in objective-plan 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→When `/erk:objective-plan` completes planning, the `exit-plan-mode-hook` fires and presents 3 options: "Create a plan PR", "Skip PR and implement here", and "View/Edit the plan". The user wants objective-plan to always direct toward saving the plan as a PR, not offering direct implementation. This makes sense because objective plans are multi-node workflows where each node becomes a tracked PR — implementing directly bypasses the tracking. 6→ 7→## Changes 8→ 9→### 1. `.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md` 10→ 11→Add a new section at the end (after "Important Notes") that overrides the hook's default behavior: 12→ 13→```markdown 14→## Post-Plan Behavior 15→ 16→**CRITICAL: Objective plans must ALWAYS be saved as PRs.** When the exit-plan-mode hook presents save-or-implement options, ALWAYS proceed with "Create a plan PR" by running `/erk:plan-save`. Do NOT offer to implement directly — objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly. 17→``` 18→ 19→Also update the Output Format section's "End" line to reinforce this. 20→ 21→### 2. `.claude/commands/erk/system/objective-plan-node.md` 22→ 23→Update **Step 8** to remove mention of the save-or-implement choice and instead say to always save: 24→ 25→- Current: "After the plan is approved in plan mode, the `exit-plan-mode-hook` will prompt to save or implement." 26→- New: Instruct to always save — when the hook fires, proceed directly with `/erk:plan-save`. 27→ 28→Also update the Output Format's "End" line. 29→ 30→## Files Modified
... [16 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Good, I understand the plan. Let me read both files that need modification, load the relevant tripwires, and signal GitHub started — all in parallel.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Create an implementation plan from an objective node 3→argument-hint: "<objective-number-or-url> [--node <node-id>]" 4→allowed-tools: Bash, Task, Skill, AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode 5→--- 6→ 7→# /erk:objective-plan 8→ 9→Create an implementation plan for a specific node in an objective's dependency graph. 10→ 11→## Usage 12→ 13→```bash 14→/erk:objective-plan 3679 15→/erk:objective-plan 3679 --node 2.1 16→/erk:objective-plan https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 17→/erk:objective-plan # prompts for objective reference 18→``` 19→ 20→--- 21→ 22→## Agent Instructions 23→ 24→### Step 0: Check for Known Node (Fast Path) 25→ 26→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for `--node <node-id>`. If `--node` is present along with an issue number: 27→ 28→**Invoke the inner skill immediately** via the Skill tool: 29→ 30→```
... [209 lines omitted] ...
110→ - If 'erk-plan' label instead: return error "This is an erk-plan PR, not an objective" 111→ - If neither label: include warning but proceed 132→WARNINGS: <any warnings about labels, roadmap format, etc., or "none"> 159→"ERROR: objective-context marker not created. Re-run the marker command manually: 215→## Error Cases 219→| Objective not found | Report error and exit |

1→--- 2→description: Create an implementation plan for a known objective node (inner skill) 3→argument-hint: "<objective-number> --node <node-id>" 4→allowed-tools: Bash, Task, Skill, AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode 5→--- 6→ 7→# /erk:system:objective-plan-node 8→ 9→Inner skill for creating an implementation plan when the objective issue and node ID are already known. Called by the outer `/erk:objective-plan` command or directly by `plan_cmd.py` via Claude launch. 10→ 11→This skips interactive node selection (Steps 4-5 of the outer command) since the node is already determined. 12→ 13→## Usage 14→ 15→```bash 16→/erk:system:objective-plan-node 3679 --node 2.1 17→``` 18→ 19→Both `<objective-number>` and `--node <node-id>` are required. 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Agent Instructions 24→ 25→### Step 1: Parse Arguments 26→ 27→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract: 28→ 29→- **Objective number**: The numeric objective reference (required) 30→- **Node ID**: The `--node` value (required)
... [181 lines omitted] ...
32→If either is missing, STOP and report: "ERROR: Both objective number and --node are required. Usage: /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id>" 51→ - If 'erk-plan' label instead: return error "This is an erk-plan PR, not an objective" 52→ - If neither label: include warning but proceed 73→WARNINGS: <any warnings about labels, roadmap format, etc., or "none"> 96→"ERROR: objective-context marker not created. Re-run the marker command manually: 186→## Error Cases 190→| Missing objective number or node ID | Report error with usage instructions | 191→| Objective not found | Report error and exit | 193→| Node not found in roadmap | Report error and list available nodes | 194→| Marker creation fails | Report error with manual command |

1→--- 2→title: Commands Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on commands code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from commands/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Commands Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**adding allowed-tools to a command or agent frontmatter** → Read [Tool Restriction Safety Pattern](tool-restriction-safety.md) first. ALWAYS apply the minimal-set principle — only allow tools the command actually needs 16→ 17→**creating a destructive slash command without a preview variant** → Read [Preview Command Pattern](preview-command-pattern.md) first. Consider pairing with a preview command (e.g., pr-address + pr-preview-address). Preview commands show planned actions without executing, reducing costly mistakes. 18→ 19→**creating commands that delegate to subagents** → Read [Tool Restriction Safety Pattern](tool-restriction-safety.md) first. NEVER omit Task from allowed-tools if the command delegates to subagents 20→ 21→**modifying collateral finding categories or auto-apply behavior in audit-doc** → Read [Audit-Doc Design Decisions](audit-doc.md) first. CRITICAL: Read this doc first to understand the conceptual vs mechanical finding distinction 22→ 23→**renaming any file in .claude/commands/ or .claude/skills/** → Read [Command Rename Pattern](command-rename-pattern.md) first. Read this doc — renames require a full reference sweep, not just a file move 24→ 25→**using CLAUDE_SESSION_ID in hooks or Python code** → Read [Session ID Substitution](session-id-substitution.md) first. CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is NOT an environment variable — it is a string substitution performed by Claude Code's skill/command loader. Treating it as an env var in hooks or Python code will silently produce an empty string. 26→ 27→**writing allowed-tools frontmatter** → Read [Tool Restriction Safety Pattern](tool-restriction-safety.md) first. Commands and agents use DIFFERENT allowed-tools syntax — check the format section 28→
<system-reminder>

205→## Output Format 206→ 207→- **Start:** "Loading objective #<number>..." 208→- **After parsing:** Display roadmap nodes with status 209→- **After selection:** "Creating plan for node <step-id>: <description>" 210→- **In plan mode:** Show plan content 211→- **End:** Always proceed with `/erk:plan-save` — do not offer direct implementation 212→ 213→--- 214→ 215→## Error Cases 216→ 217→| Scenario | Action | 218→| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | 219→| Objective not found | Report error and exit | 220→| Reference is erk-plan | Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement` | 221→| No pending nodes | Report all nodes complete, suggest closing | 222→| Invalid argument format | Prompt for valid issue number | 223→| Roadmap not parseable | Ask user to specify which node to plan | 224→| Verification fails (no objective_issue) | `/erk:plan-save` handles automatically; follow remediation steps | 225→ 226→--- 227→ 228→## Important Notes 229→ 230→- **Objective context matters:** Read the full objective for design decisions and lessons learned 231→- **One node at a time:** Each plan should focus on a single roadmap node 232→- **Link back:** Always reference the parent objective in the plan 233→- **Steelthread pattern:** If planning a Phase A node, focus on minimal vertical slice 234→

145→- Files to modify 146→- Test requirements 147→ 148→### Step 8: Save Plan as PR (Always Save) 149→ 150→After the plan is approved in plan mode, **always save as a PR** — do not offer direct implementation. When the `exit-plan-mode-hook` fires, proceed directly with `/erk:plan-save`. Objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly. 151→ 152→The objective-context marker created in Step 2 is automatically read by `/erk:plan-save`. Simply run `/erk:plan-save` and it will link the plan to the objective. 153→ 154→**If the marker was not created (fallback):** 155→Create it manually before saving: 156→ 157→```bash 158→erk exec marker create --session-id "${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" --associated-objective <objective-number> objective-context 159→``` 160→ 161→Then run `/erk:plan-save`. 162→ 163→### Step 9: Verify Objective Link 164→ 165→After saving, the JSON output includes `objective_issue`. Check that it matches the expected objective number. 166→ 167→If verification is needed: 168→ 169→```bash 170→erk exec get-plan-metadata <new-plan-number> objective_issue 171→``` 172→ 173→Check that `value` matches the expected objective number. 174→

<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
session.assistant: This plan only modifies markdown files (slash commands), so there's no code to lint/test. But let me run `make fast-ci` as directed to confirm nothing is broken.

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` and report results. Only report the output, do not fix anything.

session.tool_result:
## make fast-ci Results
**Status: FAILED (Exit Code 2)**
### Summary
The `make fast-ci` command failed due to **Prettier formatting issues**, but all other checks and tests passed:
#### Passing Checks: - **Lint**: All checks passed - **ruff**: All checks passed - **ty**: All checks passed - **Format Check**: 1626 files already formatted - **Markdown Check**: OK - **Exec Reference Check**: OK (.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md is up to date) - **Unit Tests (erk)**: 5485 passed in 21.61s - **Unit Tests (erk-dev)**: 136 passed in 4.48s - **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)**: 112 passed in 0.85s - **Unit Tests (erkbot)**: 114 passed in 2.20s
#### Failing Check: - **Prettier Check**: Code style issues found in 2 files: - `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
These 2 markdown files need formatting fixes. The make target fails because Prettier requires all files to be properly formatted. agentId: a88775f (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
session.assistant: The prettier failures are in unrelated files (`docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` and `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`), not in the files I modified. Let me fix them quickly to get a clean CI.

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `prettier --write docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` and report the results.

session.tool_result:
prettier passed: 2 files formatted successfully
**Summary:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` formatted in 67ms - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` formatted in 23ms
Both files have been formatted with Prettier code style. agentId: a25ba2f (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` and report results. Only report the output, do not fix anything.

session.tool_result:
make fast-ci passed: All checks and tests passed successfully.
Summary: - Lint: All checks passed - Format Check: 1626 files already formatted - Prettier Check: All matched files use Prettier code style - Markdown Check: OK - Exec Reference Check: OK - ty (Type checking): All checks passed - Unit Tests (erk): 5485 passed in 11.57s - Tests (erk-dev): 136 passed in 4.36s - Tests (erk-statusline): 112 passed in 0.78s - Tests (erkbot): 114 passed in 1.59s - AGENTS.md standard: PASSED - CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md pairing: 19 files checked, 0 violations agentId: a102b10 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.assistant:
Plan #8586 execution complete.
**Summary of changes:**
1. **`.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md`** — Added "Post-Plan Behavior" section after "Important Notes" with a CRITICAL directive to always save as PR via `/erk:plan-save`. Updated Output Format "End" line to reinforce always-save.
2. **`.claude/commands/erk/system/objective-plan-node.md`** — Renamed Step 8 to "Save Plan as PR (Always Save)" and updated its text to instruct always proceeding with `/erk:plan-save` instead of offering a save-or-implement choice. Updated Output Format "End" line to match.
The exit-plan-mode hook itself was not modified — the command-level instructions override the agent's behavior when it sees the hook's options.
**PR:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8586
